title: Forge project composition (.forge/composition.json)
description: Per-active-root project state: the set of resources this project has ADOPTED from its catalog READ-VIEW. A project's COMPOSITION is its declared working set, distinct from the global git-tracked LIBRARY that admission curates (ADR-0019). An adopted entry is keyed by the pair (uid, sourceId): `uid` is the resource uid "<kind>:<id>" (ADR-0005) and `sourceId` is the source id it was adopted from, or null for the library-local copy. ADOPT != ADMIT: adoption records a per-project selection and does NOT run the admission pipeline, consult the T2 gate, or write the library (the catalog admit -> library path is UNCHANGED). Adoptability is gated by the ADR-0018 read-view (library-local records UNION subscribed-slice records); a resource must be visible to be adopted. A newly visible resource defaults UNADOPTED (opt-in, consistent with slices defaulting unsubscribed, ADR-0018, and sources defaulting untrusted, ADR-0017). Owned by manager/compose.mjs (forge compose list|adopt|remove). Writes are ADDITIVE and never destructive (writeJsonAtomic via manager/lib/store.mjs); adopt/remove only add/drop one (uid, sourceId) entry. An entry whose resource is no longer in the read-view (orphan) is REPORTED (WARN), never silently deleted. This file lives UNDER the active project root, not in the git-tracked library, and is created on first --apply.

schema.description: On-disk schema tag for this file.

_note.description: Free-form authoring note; ignored by the composition operator.

adopted.description: The resources this project has adopted from its read-view. Each entry is the pair (uid, sourceId). An absent (uid, sourceId) pair is UNADOPTED. Order is not significant; entries SHOULD be unique on (uid, sourceId) (the operator dedups and sorts on write — by uid, then sourceId).

adoptedEntry.description: One adopted resource, identified by the pair (uid, sourceId). The same uid adopted from the library (sourceId === null) and from a source are two DISTINCT entries.

uid.description: A resource uid "<kind>:<id>": the singular registry kind (agent|skill|command|rule|hook|bundle|validator|mcp|meta-test|engine) and the resource id, joined by a single colon (ADR-0005 / the registry record).

sourceId.description: The source the resource was adopted from: the source id (manifests/sources.json#sources[].id), or null for the library-local copy (an owned in-tree record, source === null, ADR-0017).
